title: Separate published and candidate release metadata

sources.reason: Work Item 8 requires published/candidate metadata separation and Quick Install isolation.
Published release metadata is the only public install input.
Quick Install ref and source selection behavior.
Release preparation and public distribution validation.
Document candidate metadata and release preparation boundaries.

riskAssessment.reason: Quick Install must never consume candidate metadata or an unreleased tag.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete review plan and release metadata changes are bounded by this Contract.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Quick Install with a candidate file present
Candidate preparation without public release mutation
Published release tag, digest, and manifest consistency

acceptance: Quick Install and installer source metadata use only the published `release.json` and never consume `next-release.json`.
Release preparation validates `next-release.json` as exactly the next patch candidate without changing the published release contract.
Regression tests and documentation prove published tag, digest, manifest, and installation entry consistency.
guidelines: Published release metadata remains immutable from the candidate preparation path.
Quick Install must not consume candidate metadata or moving work branches.
Document the published-versus-candidate boundary and verification behavior.

intent.problem: The repository has one release metadata path and release preparation currently relies on changing that path, which can make Quick Install consume an unreleased candidate.
constraints: Keep `release.json` as the published public contract used by Quick Install.
Candidate metadata must be explicit, separate, and rejected as a public install source.
intent.rationale: Separating `next-release.json` from the published contract makes candidate validation possible without contaminating the stable installation entry.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete review plan, including release, installer, CI, branch, PR, merge, and cleanup work.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
